items.title: Knicks given surprising offseason grade after winning championship
items.summary: Apr 30, 2026; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA; Philadelphia 76ers center Andre Drummond (1) reacts to his score against the Boston Celtics during the second quarter at Xfinity Mobile Arena. Mandatory Credit: Bill Streicher-Imagn Images | Bill Streicher-Imagn Images The New York Knicks are still riding the high of winning the NBA Finals over the San Antonio Spurs last month.The offseason has brought difficult choices for the team to make, despite ending a 53-year championship drought. The team said goodbye to Mitchell Robinson, who signed a three-year contract with the Boston Celtics. Because of this, CBS Sports contributor Sam Quinn gave the Knicks a C-plus for their offseason grade so far.“The Knicks will have to figure something else out at center. They don’t even have a third big yet. Maybe they find someone at training camp. Maybe they trade for someone during the season. They tried…

items.title: The Spurs enjoyed the last quiet offseason they’ll have for a while
items.summary: Apr 28, 2026; San Antonio, Texas, USA; San Antonio Spurs general manager Brian Wright before game five of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs against the Portland Trail Blazers at Frost Bank Center. Mandatory Credit: Scott Wachter-Imagn Images | Scott Wachter-Imagn Images The Spurs had a rather boring offseason. They loaded up on size at the draft, they retained Harrison Barnes and Jordan McLaughlin, and their biggest addition was 34-year-old Tobias Harris. Victor Wembanyama got a no-brainer of a max contract, and that was it. A solid offseason for a young team coming off a Finals run, but not one filled with major decisions and big swings.This will likely be the last summer when the front office will get the privilege of not having to make sacrifices, deal with potentially contentious negotiations, and have to make plays for the future. By next offseason, San Antonio might find…

items.title: The 5 reasons why LeBron James is taking so long to make his decision
items.summary: lebron james at fanatics fan fest and with lakersThe World Cup is over. But LeBron James watch continues. Why is this taking so long, you may be asking.  Speculation around where James will play his unprecedented 24th NBA season has taken over the newscycle for weeks. Multiple teams are frozen waiting for his decision. Even NBA commissioner Adam Silver recently urged James to make his pick so the league can finalize the schedule. James is almost 42 years old, but 27 teams have shown interest in having him on their roster. The league has ground to a halt until he makes his choice. Talk about still having pull. Here are the five reasons why James may be taking so long to make his decision.1. Because LeBron Can When you’re arguably the greatest player of all-time, different rules apply. James has devoted himself to the sport for 23 years.…

items.title: Lakers add Matisse Thybulle to fill weak spot, but create roster crunch
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers are making a low-stakes bet on Matisse Thybulle.The 29-year-old wing known for his elite defensive skills is the latest addition to the Lakers' offseason roster overhaul, according to ESPN, after agreeing a one-year deal with the team on Monday, July 20.Thybulle is seen as a potential fit as part of the Lakers' wing rotation due to his versatility as a defender and a 3-point shot that improved since earlier in his career. He has also struggled to remain healthy in recent seasons and his addition leaves the Lakers with at least one player too many under contract heading into the 2026-27 campaign. Yet it's still far from certain Thybulle can be a viable option alongside Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves.Here's what to know about Thybulle's signing with the Lakers, including contract details, the team's current roster and what it means for the franchise moving…

NOTE TO USER: User expressly acknowledges and agrees that, by downloading and or using this photograph, User is consenting to the terms and conditions of the Getty Images License Agreement. (Photo by Harry How/Getty Images) | Getty Images The Cleveland Cavaliers, like everyone else, are patiently (and anxiously) awaiting LeBron James’s latest decision. It’s been roughly four weeks since James announced he would not be returning to the Los Angeles Lakers. The King will be moving on after eight years wearing the purple and gold. And now, we are all still waiting for him to announce where his talents will be taken…

items.title: LeBron James’ free agency decision timeline gets frustrating update
items.summary: New York, NY July 16, 2026 LeBron James made multiple high-profile appearances at Fanatics Fest NYC 2026, including a live recording of his Mind the Game podcast with Indiana Pacers...The ball is in LeBron James’ court, and he intends to keep it there longer than anyone would like.The entire basketball world is waiting with decreasing patience for James to announce which team he’ll join in free agency. While there were some rumors that LeBron’s decision could arrive as soon as Monday, an update from ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania revealed that the wait will continue.LeBron James speaks during Fanatics Fest in New York City. BACKGRID“I spoke to Rich Paul, [James’] longtime agent, this morning. And Rich Paul made it clear to me that the choice is LeBron James’ right now… There is no decision, no choice yet. There’s no timetable as of yet,” Charania said, per an X post from ESPN Cleveland…

items.title: A List Of NBA Draft Picks Out Of Villanova
items.summary: Villanova has enjoyed great success and put itself in the conversation of blue blood territory in recent years.This success dates back decades as the Wildcats have been sending players to the next level since 1950.That year, Paul Arizin was selected third-overall by the Philadelphia Warriors. To this day, he remains as the highest-drafted player to come out of Villanova.Arizin was also the first of nine players selected in the Top 10 in the NBA Draft and one of 66 total players. The 2018 class had the most selections with four. Mikal Bridges led the way that year as the 10th overall selection while Donte DiVincenzo and Omari Spellman joined him in the first round. Jalen Brunson went on to be a second round selection as well.With the addition of Luigi Suigo and others, it looks as though the list will grow in 2027 but for now, this is how it looks:YearRoundPickPlayerTeam195013Paul…

items.title: LeBron James tells Patrick Beverley to ‘Shut the f— up’ with Kevin Hart’s help
items.summary: lebron james at fanatics fan fest cropped next to comedian kevin hartJust hours after Patrick Beverley posted to social media that he heard LeBron James would be making his decision on who he will play for next season, James opted to troll his former teammate.James posted a video on his Instagram story of famous comedian and actor Kevin Hart saying, “Shut the f— up,” in reaction to his former teammate. That was followed by him leaving a comment that said “BINGO STFU.”“I don’t know who needs to hear this, but I feel like it’s important. I feel like somebody needs to hear this: shut the f— up,” Hart said in the video, which is followed up by him repeating the phrase over and over again.LeBron just reposted Kevin Hart’s reel on his IG story saying “STFU” #LeBron#Sixerspic.twitter.com/9Vz4paKtc7— Mike (@sixersjumper) July 20, 2026James’ Instagram story post came after Beverley tweeted that…

items.summary: Could Pat Riley welcome back LeBron James with open arms if he decided to sign with the Miami Heat during NBA free agency?Riley appeared as a guest on “The Dan LeBatard Show” on Monday morning and talked about how he’s let go of the past and the current state of his relationship with James.“I’ve seen him around, run into him at All-Star games, we had a great embrace when they honored me with a statue out there,” Riley said. “Seeing he and Savannah in the stands at certain things, she’s always been a really gracious, wonderful person.”Pat Riley on his relationship with LeBron James:“I've seen him around and run into him at All-Star Games.
